commit.message:
Add ElfSymbolModule for Parsing ELF Symbol Tables (#2384)

* Add ElfSymbolModule for parsing ELF symbol tables

Add ElfSymbolModule, which reads ELF (Executable and Linkable Format)
files and resolves RVAs to symbol names. This enables PerfView and
TraceEvent to resolve native symbols from Linux ELF binaries when
analyzing universal traces captured on Linux.

Key capabilities:
- Parses both .symtab and .dynsym sections
- Supports 32-bit and 64-bit ELF, little-endian and big-endian
- Implements ISymbolLookup for integration with TraceEvent's symbol
  resolution pipeline
- Demangles Itanium C++ (_Z) and Rust v0 (_R) mangled names

Design for performance:
- Lazy name resolution: symbol names decoded on first FindNameForRva
  hit, not during construction
- LOH-free strtab via SegmentedList<byte> with 64KB segments
- Two-pass section scan pre-allocates all structures with zero resizes
- BitConverter fast path for 64-bit LE symbol entries
- O(log n) zero-allocation lookups via sorted array binary search

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ElfSymbolModule unit tests

Add comprehensive test suite with 26 tests covering all ElfSymbolModule
code paths:
- Error handling: invalid magic, truncated, empty, bad class, no sections
- Format coverage: 64-bit LE, 32-bit LE, 64-bit BE, 32-bit BE
- Symbol filtering: non-function types, zero-value, zero-size, below PT_LOAD
- RVA adjustment with pVaddr and pOffset
- Demangling integration: Itanium C++, Rust v0, plain passthrough
- .dynsym section parsing alongside .symtab
- Edge cases: empty table, RVA zero, 100-symbol binary search stress
- File path constructor validation

ElfBuilder is a test helper that constructs synthetic minimal ELF
binaries in memory, avoiding the need for checked-in test binaries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ElfSymbolModule benchmarks

Add BenchmarkDotNet benchmarks for ELF symbol parsing and lookup using
ElfBuilder-generated synthetic ELF binaries (5, 256, and 10000 symbols).
No external file dependencies.

Parse benchmarks measure construction time and memory allocation.
Lookup benchmarks measure FindNameForRva performance (zero-alloc).

Link ElfBuilder.cs from the test project to share the ELF binary
builder without duplicating code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add premium AI-powered stock analytics dashboard (#29)

* Initial plan

* Add premium dashboard SQL migration and API routes

- Create premium tables migration (trades, user_levels, ai_insights, portfolio_holdings)
- Add Flask blueprint with 11 API endpoints for dashboard features
- Includes portfolio overview, market indices, AI insights, sentiment analysis,
  sector heatmap, trade recording, user gamification levels, and more

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix badges index and PnL calculation in premium dashboard routes

- Fix row index for badges field (row[7] not row[6]) in user_level endpoint
- Fix PnL calculation to use explicit parameters instead of referencing
  column value being updated in the same SET clause
- Also update pnl_percent when recalculating portfolio on BUY

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive premium dark theme CSS for stock analytics dashboard

CRED/Bloomberg-inspired OLED-optimized dark theme with:
- CSS variables for profit/loss/AI/premium colors and design tokens
- Glassmorphism effects with backdrop-filter blur
- Inter font family with large bold tabular numbers
- Collapsible sidebar with icon+label navigation
- Top header bar with search, profile, and notifications
- Hero portfolio card with animated value display
- Market index mini cards with sparkline areas
- AI prediction cards with confidence-based glowing borders
- Watchlist table and card layouts
- AI Insights premium notification feed
- Floating AI chat widget (bottom-right)
- Gamification badges, XP progress bars, achievements
- Sector heatmap grid
- Recent activity timeline
- Stock detail panel with chart area and stats
- Sentiment gauge component
- Trade modal styles
- Toast notification system
- Loading skeleton and pulse animations
- Animated counter digit effects
- Responsive breakpoints: tablet (1024px), mobile (640px), small (380px)
- Custom scrollbar, reduced-motion support, touch-device tweaks
- Gradient text, gradient borders, hover effects, utility classes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive premium dashboard JavaScript

Implements full-featured premium dashboard with:
- Portfolio overview with animated counters (Indian ₹ format)
- Market indices with sparkline charts via Chart.js
- AI predictions panel with confidence bars and glow effects
- Watchlist table with mini trend charts
- AI insights feed with type-coded notification cards
- Recent activity timeline with Buy/Sell color coding
- Sector heatmap grid with performance coloring
- Sentiment gauge (bullish/bearish/neutral)
- User level/gamification with XP bar and badges
- Debounced stock search with autocomplete dropdown
- Floating AI chat widget with history
- Trade recording modal with validation
- Socket.IO real-time updates (predictions, prices, watchlist)
- Sidebar navigation with smooth scroll
- Loading skeletons and toast notifications
- Auto-refresh of market data every 60 seconds
- Time-based greeting
- XSS-safe HTML escaping throughout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix dead code and use data-attributes for search results

Address code review feedback:
- Remove dead conditional in hideSkeleton, replace with active skeleton cleanup
- Replace inline onclick/onmouseover handlers in search results with
  data-attribute + event delegation pattern for safer XSS handling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add premium dashboard HTML template

Create app/templates/premium_dashboard.html — a production-ready Flask/Jinja2
template for the StockSense premium AI-powered dashboard.

- Left sidebar with icon navigation and data-section attributes for JS routing
- Top header with search bar, notifications, AI assistant toggle, profile dropdown
- Hero portfolio card with animated value display, P&L, and sparkline canvas
- Live market indices grid with skeleton loading states
- AI predictions panel with confidence scoring layout
- Market sentiment gauge with SVG ring visualization
- Sector heatmap grid container
- Watchlist cards with mini chart placeholders
- AI insights feed with typed insight cards
- Portfolio holdings section with Add Trade CTA
- Recent activity timeline
- Floating AI chat widget with message area and input
- Trade modal form with BUY/SELL toggle, quantity, and price fields
- Toast notification container
- All element IDs match premium_dashboard.js expectations
- All CSS classes align with premium.css definitions
- External CDN: Inter font, Font Awesome 6.4.0, Chart.js 4.4.0, Socket.IO 4.5.4
- No inline CSS or JavaScript — all styles/logic in external files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add premium AI-powered dashboard with backend APIs, CSS, JS, and HTML template

feat(temporal): add Phase 4 tests, update CMake/CI/ROADMAP

- Add test_interval_tree_index.cpp: 15 tests for augmented interval tree
  (insert/size, queryPoint half-open semantics, queryOverlap, queryKey,
  remove/removeKey/clear, stats tracking, name())
- Add test_temporal_compressor.cpp: 9 tests for DELTA/ZSTD/Gorilla/Dictionary
  compression (algorithmName, empty-table stats, decompress passthrough,
  versions_compressed > 0, versions_skipped grace window)
- Add test_temporal_cdc.cpp: 16 tests for subscribe/publish/replay CDC
  (lifecycle, table-scoped vs wildcard delivery, replayChanges, JSON
  round-trip, ring-buffer overflow eviction)
- cmake/CMakeLists.txt: register 3 new source files + 3 gtest targets
- cmake/ModularBuild.cmake: register 3 new source files
- .github/workflows/temporal-phase4-ci.yml: new CI workflow (gcc-12 + clang-15)
- src/temporal/ROADMAP.md: Phase 4 → Partial ⚙️, mark 3 items [x],
  update Known Issues

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add doc-sync workflow for automated documentation PR generation (#4832)

* Sync workflow to detect CLI changes and open docs PRs

* Rename secret to AZURE_DOCS_REPO_PAT

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add PAT expiry reminder workflow

Monthly cron job that opens a GitHub Issue 30 days before the
AZURE_DOCS_REPO_PAT expires. Prevents silent doc-sync failures
due to expired credentials. Deduplicates — won't open a second
issue if one is already open.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace PAT reminder with failure-triggered issue

Instead of a separate cron workflow, the doc-sync workflow now
opens a GitHub Issue automatically when it fails to push a PR
to the docs repo. The issue includes troubleshooting steps for
expired PATs, permission issues, and branch conflicts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive self-healing for multi-agent groups lost on restart (#412)

## Problem
Multi-agent groups were silently lost across app restarts. The
`organization.json` on disk had:
- All groups with `IsMultiAgent=false`
- All sessions with `Role=Worker` (even orchestrator sessions)
- Multi-agent group IDs from event-diagnostics didn't exist in the file

Affected teams: PR Review Squad, MAUI - PR Reviewer, FixWeirdInfoPList,
PP- Organize, Evaluate Ortinau Skills, Implement & Challenge (6 teams
total, ~25 sessions).

## Root Cause
The self-healing in `LoadOrganization` only checked `Role ==
Orchestrator`, but all sessions defaulted to `Worker` when the org file
was stale. Chicken-and-egg: can't detect orchestrator → can't heal group
→ can't protect sessions → sessions scatter.

## Fix
Three-phase self-healing in `HealMultiAgentGroups()`:

1. **Phase 1**: Detect orchestrator sessions by name pattern
(`*-orchestrator*`) and restore `Role=Orchestrator`
2. **Phase 2**: Restore `IsMultiAgent` on groups that have orchestrator
sessions (with name matching to avoid incorrectly marking repo groups)
3. **Phase 3**: Reconstruct missing multi-agent groups from scattered
sessions by detecting team members via name prefix patterns

Also adds save verification and error logging to
`event-diagnostics.log`.

## Tests
6 new tests covering:
- Orchestrator role detection by name pattern (with suffix variants)
- Group reconstruction from scattered sessions
- Multiple scattered teams handling
- No false positives on regular session names
- No-op when groups are already correct

All 55 organization tests pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add .gitattributes to normalize line endings

Fixes phantom CRLF/LF diffs when working across Windows and WSL.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v1.6.0: Security hardening & bug fixes

- Fix path traversal in save-config and config-history-content (critical)
- Fix SFTP path traversal with _validate_remote_filename helper
- Replace SSH AutoAddPolicy with WarningPolicy, return host fingerprint
- Fix ZeroSSL EAB bug: add separate MAC key field
- Add JSON body validation to all 12 POST endpoints
- Add git files list regex validation
- Fix unhealthy_request_count -> unhealthy_latency directive
- Use gunicorn in Docker instead of Flask dev server
- install.sh uses requirements.txt instead of hardcoded deps
- Internationalize all validation messages (8 strings)
- Update RELEASE_NOTES.md and README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix iOS TestFlight build: remove hardcoded signing identity, clean up plist injection (#411)

## Problem
The iOS build has been failing on TestFlight CI ([run
#23331578098](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/23331578098/job/67864110605))
with:

```
Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8): no identity found
error MSB3073: codesign --force --sign "Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8)" ... exited with code 1.
```

## Root Cause
The csproj had a post-build target (`InjectIOSPrivacyKeys`) with a
**hardcoded codesign exec** using Jakub Florkowski's signing identity.
This was part of a workaround that re-injected NS*UsageDescription
privacy keys into Info.plist after build, then re-signed the app.

## Fix
**Removed the entire PlistBuddy injection mechanism** (both
`InjectMacCatalystPrivacyKeys` and `InjectIOSPrivacyKeys` targets + the
hardcoded codesign).

The injection was a workaround for an old MAUI behavior that stripped
`NS*UsageDescription` keys from the output Info.plist. This is no longer
the case — verified with a fully clean build (`rm -rf bin/ obj/`) on
.NET 10 that the `CompileAppManifest` task in
[`dotnet/macios`](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs)
now preserves all source plist keys.

The privacy keys (`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`,
`NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`) are already defined in both
`Platforms/iOS/Info.plist` and `Platforms/MacCatalyst/Info.plist` and
survive the build pipeline untouched.

## Verification
- ✅ Full clean build (bin/ + obj/ nuked) on Mac Catalyst — 0 errors
- ✅ Privacy keys present in output `.app/Contents/Info.plist` without
any injection
- ✅ Confirmed `CompileAppManifest` [source
code](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs#L105)
reads full source plist and doesn't strip NS* keys

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Apply security fixes to server.py

- FIX 1: Path traversal protection in /api/save-config with hostname sanitization and containment check
- FIX 2: Path traversal protection in /api/config-history-content with .bak extension and containment check
- FIX 3: SFTP path traversal protection via _validate_remote_filename helper
- FIX 4: Replace AutoAddPolicy with WarningPolicy and expose host key fingerprint
- FIX 5: Null JSON body validation on all POST endpoints
- FIX 6: Git files list validation with safe filename regex

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add unit tests for doc-cache.ts (#8136)

* Add unit tests for doc-cache.ts

Test coverage for DocumentCache class including:
- getCachedRxDocuments: caching, deduplication, multiple docs, lwt differentiation
- getCachedRxDocument: single doc caching
- getLatestDocumentData/getLatestDocumentDataIfExists: retrieval and error handling
- Change stream updates: latestDoc updates, fallback to previousDocumentData
- processTasks: task execution and clearing
- cacheItemByDocId: cache entry creation, multi-revision storage
- mapDocumentsDataToCacheDocs: helper function delegation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SRfcwhBthfMnTLXheFnMFM

commit.message:
refactor: make Astro WordPress APIs client-first (#27)

* feat: unify auth bridge with fluent-wp-client patterns

- Refactor WordPressAuthBridgeConfig to extend WordPressClientConfig
- Add support for auth, authHeader, authHeaders configuration options
- Implement authResolver for custom context-aware auth resolution
- Update getActionAuth(), resolveUser(), isAuthenticated() to be async
- Use fluent-wp-client's createAuthResolver and resolveWordPressAuth patterns
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing cookie-based JWT sessions
- Update test fixtures to work with new async methods
- Update documentation with unified auth configuration examples

* feat: add getClientConfig() to auth bridge for fluent-wp-client compatibility

- Add getClientConfig() method that returns complete WordPressClientConfig
- Config can be used directly with new WordPressClient() or spread into actions
- Deprecate getActionAuth() in favor of the new unified getClientConfig()
- Keep getActionAuth() for backward compatibility
- Update WordPressAuthBridge interface with baseUrl property
- Update documentation with middleware and fluent-wp-client integration examples
- Add complete examples showing bridge usage with both package actions and fluent-wp-client
- All 79 integration tests pass

* refactor: make loaders and actions client-first

Use WordPressClient instances and request-aware client resolvers across Astro loaders and action factories. This removes duplicated auth/config plumbing so the package stays aligned with fluent-wp-client and the auth bridge.

* docs: remove migration-style wording from auth docs

* Update src/server/auth.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: finish client-first auth bridge API

Remove the legacy getActionAuth shim so the bridge resolves request auth through client-first helpers like the rest of the package. Preserve static auth fallback handling so bridge-created clients keep working across supported auth inputs.

* Update src/server/auth.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/actions/ability/delete.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/actions/ability/execute.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/actions/ability/get.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make auth bridge static fallback opt-in

Keep resolveUser and isAuthenticated focused on request-derived user state while still allowing explicit service-client fallback through getClient and getClientConfig.

* refactor: trim auth bridge fallback surface

Keep the opt-in static fallback behavior while reducing the auth bridge type surface to the API shape callers actually need.

* fix: respect auth bridge client authHeaders

Let per-call authHeaders override bridge-level authHeaders without allowing resolved auth fields to be replaced, and clean up the users action docs snippet.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
CaddyConfer v1.5.0 - Initial release

Web-based configuration generator for Caddy server reverse proxy.

Features:
- 12 accordion sections for complete Caddy config generation
- Live preview with syntax highlighting and validation
- Recommendations engine with auto-fix actions
- SSH/SFTP remote deployment (upload, validate, reload)
- Git push integration
- Certificate generation (CA + client + PFX)
- Full i18n (Swedish/English)
- Dark mode
- Inline Caddy documentation links
- Config history and versioning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix UI/API integration: centralize API service layer, fix broken endpoints, replace CDN assets (#28)

* Initial plan

* fix: centralize API service layer, fix endpoint mismatches, fix static paths, improve error handling

- Create app/static/services/api.js with StockAPI, PredictionAPI, WatchlistAPI,
  AlertAPI, NotificationAPI, SystemAPI, PriceStreamAPI, ChatAPI + showToast/setButtonLoading helpers
- Fix dashboard.js: /api/predictions?page= → /api/predictions/?page= (trailing slash)
- Fix alerts_mgmt.html, notifications.html, admin_system.html: hardcoded /static/ paths
  replaced with url_for('static', ...) per codebase convention; load services/api.js
- Fix stocks_list.html: replace CDN Bootstrap/FA links with local static assets;
  include services/api.js
- Rewrite alerts_mgmt.js using AlertAPI with loading states and toast notifications
- Rewrite admin_system.js using SystemAPI with Promise.all(), loading states, toasts
- Rewrite alerts.js using NotificationAPI with improved error display
- Update stocks_list.js to use StockAPI.getList() instead of raw fetch()

commit.message:
Streamline pipeline: remove params.outdir overrides and consolidate shared processes

Task 1: Remove module-level params.outdir overrides from main.nf and all
affected module files (canoes, clamms, cnvkit, gatk-gcnv, evaluate,
feature-extraction, normalise, survivor, train, truvari). Also removed
unused outdir.mkdir() calls from main.nf and modules-evaluate.nf.

Task 2: Create modules/modules-common.nf with shared BGZIP_SORT_INDEX_VCF
and NORMALISE_CNV_QUALITY_SCORES processes parametrised by tool_annot/
caller_name and dir_suffix vals.

Task 3 (partial): Updated modules-canoes, modules-clamms, modules-xhmm,
modules-cnvkit, modules-indelible, and modules-gatk-gcnv to include from
modules-common.nf, remove duplicate process blocks, and pass the correct
caller/dir_suffix arguments in workflow calls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: nf-multicaller-exomecnv
repository.description: The nf-multicaller-exomecnv workflow enables the calling of CNVs using CANOES, CLAMMS, CNVkit, Illumina's DRAGEN Germline Enrichment, GATK-gCNV and INDELIBLE, with SV merging using Truvari or SURVIVOR, and in silico Validation via a Random Forest Classifier
